mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions, marked by a sustained rise in the unemployment rate, signals collapsing consumer spending power and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, invalidating the labor-income transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series declines in isolation, or IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals stabilizing factory output and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of months with rising industrial production, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crises.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and disinflation removes real yield headwinds, allowing equity valuations to expand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when real rates stay positive despite nominal rate cuts, or IC < 0.03 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Net declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate income expectations, forcing equity price adjustments and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll declines coincide with positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations, invalidating the income-channel transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 800,000 units alongside contracting real GDP confirm a structural breakdown in the housing-led demand cycle, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when fiscal stimulus offsets private demand weakness, or forward returns turn positive in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low funding costs combined with stabilizing industrial output reduce corporate financing constraints and inventory write-down risks, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive short-term returns fail to materialize when bank lending channels remain frozen despite low policy rates, or IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
